name: e12 - Two-way vs Three-way Match Condition

nodes.name: Buyer resolves price-variance
nodes.documentation: Price-variance routed to Buyer. Resolution dropdown {accept | adjust | credit-note | debit-note | reject}. Buyer accepts the new price (amend), raises a debit note for the overcharge, requests a supplier credit note, or rejects. permission match.resolveException (price). [SCOR S2.7 | source: data-model resolution set]

nodes.name: Receiving resolves qty / missing-GR
nodes.documentation: qty-over / qty-under / missing-GR routed to Receiving. Resolution: raise/correct the GRN (missing-GR), confirm short/over receipt, accept within revised tolerance, or reject. permission match.resolveException (qty). A missing-GR on a three-way line cannot clear until a GRN is raised. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model qty/GR resolver]

nodes.name: Tax/Compliance resolves tax-mismatch
nodes.documentation: tax-mismatch routed to Tax/Compliance. Verify taxCodeId, rate, taxableAmount, reverse-charge flag for imports, recoverable input credit; correct the line tax or raise a debit/credit note. permission match.resolveException (tax). [SCOR S2.7 | source: data-model tax resolver]

nodes.name: Finance resolves duplicate-invoice
nodes.documentation: duplicate-invoice routed to Finance. Confirm it is a true duplicate (same supplier + invoice# + amount) -> reject/void it; or, if a legitimate distinct invoice with a coincidental signature, override with reason and proceed. permission match.resolveException. [SCOR S2.7 | source: build-new duplicate resolver]

nodes.name: Three-way match (PO + GRN + invoice)
nodes.documentation: Match invoice line against PO line AND GRN: compare unitPrice to agreedPrice, invoice quantity to received (GRN) quantity, and amounts including tax. Sets up the tolerance evaluation. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: build-new three-way]

nodes.name: Two-way match (PO + invoice) + milestone acceptance
nodes.documentation: Match invoice line against PO line only (no GRN), gated by milestone acceptance for service POs: the milestone must be acceptanceStatus = accepted before payment releases (paymentReleaseOnAcceptance). Compare unitPrice and amounts incl. tax; quantity check is the milestone/acceptance, not a GRN. [SCOR S2.7 | source: data-model two-way + milestone acceptance]

nodes.name: Mark matched; relieve GR/IR; commitment to actual
nodes.documentation: matchStatus = matched. Relieve the GR/IR clearing entry (status cleared) for the matched amount; move the budget commitment to actual (committed -= , actual += ). Post the payable to the creditor ledger. Audit + SSE. Hands to 10 payments. [SCOR S2.7/OE11 | ISO 8.6 | source: build-new GR/IR clearing + commitment relief]

nodes.name: Tolerance check (price%, qty%, absolute, tax-incl)
nodes.documentation: Apply MatchTolerance config: pricePercent, qtyPercent, absolute, evaluated TAX-INCLUSIVE. Within tolerance on ALL of: abs(invoiceUnitPrice - agreedPrice) <= agreedPrice * pricePercent/100; abs(invoiceQty - matchedQty) <= matchedQty * qtyPercent/100; abs(invoiceTotalIncTax - expectedTotalIncTax) <= absolute. matchedQty = GRN received qty (three-way) or PO/milestone qty (two-way). All within -> auto-clear. Any outside -> exception with a type. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model MatchTolerance]

nodes.name: Duplicate invoice?
nodes.documentation: Standard AP control BEFORE matching. Expression: exists another invoice with same supplierId AND same invoiceNumber AND same totalIncTax (the three-key duplicate signature). True -> duplicate-invoice exception, route to resolver, do NOT post. False -> proceed to match-type selection. [SCOR S2.7 | source: build-new duplicate detection supplier + invoice# + amount]

nodes.name: GRN exists for the line / category?
nodes.documentation: Determines match type. Expression: a GRN exists for the invoice line's PO line (goods-receipt category, i.e. materials). True -> THREE-WAY match (PO + GRN + invoice). False (service / freight / no-GRN category, e.g. spares received by Engineering with no GRN) -> TWO-WAY match (PO + invoice) plus milestone acceptance. Rule: three-way iff a GRN exists, else two-way. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model matchType rule]

nodes.name: Within tolerance on all checks?
nodes.documentation: Expression: priceWithin AND qtyWithin AND amountWithin (all true). True -> auto-clear: matchStatus = matched, relieve GR/IR (cleared), move commitment to actual. False -> classify the exception type. [SCOR S2.7 | source: build-new auto-clear vs exception]

nodes.name: Exception type?
nodes.documentation: Classify into one type and route to the right resolver. Types: price-variance (unitPrice out of band) -> Buyer; qty-over (invoiceQty > matchedQty + tol) -> Receiving; qty-under (invoiceQty < matchedQty - tol) -> Receiving; missing-GR (three-way expected but GRN absent/short) -> Receiving; duplicate-invoice (from the pre-check) -> Finance; tax-mismatch (taxAmount/taxCode wrong) -> Tax/Compliance. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: data-model MatchException type + routedTo]

nodes.name: Exception resolved to a match?
nodes.documentation: Expression: resolution in {accept, adjust, credit-note, debit-note} AND the adjusted figures now fall within tolerance. True -> matched (clear GR/IR, commitment to actual). False (reject) -> rejected; invoice returned to supplier / parked; no payment. [SCOR S2.7 | source: data-model match resolution outcome]

nodes.name: Invoice captured against PO
nodes.documentation: Trigger: Finance-Maker captures a supplier invoice (with documents) linked to a PO. Fields: supplierId, poId, invoiceNumber, invoiceDate, lines [{poLineId, quantity, unitPrice, taxableAmount, taxCodeId, taxAmount}], totalIncTax, attachments. The match engine runs next. [SCOR S2.7 | ISO 8.6 | source: build-new match + AB invoice package]

nodes.name: Rejected; no payment posted
nodes.documentation: Terminal reject: duplicate confirmed or exception not resolvable. No payable posted; invoice voided/returned; GR/IR remains accrued (goods-received-not-invoiced) until a corrected invoice arrives. [SCOR S2.7 | source: build-new]

nodes.name: Matched; ready for payment
nodes.documentation: Invoice matched (two-way or three-way), within tolerance or resolved; GR/IR relieved, commitment actualised, payable on the creditor ledger. Hands to 10 payments (schedule, maker/checker release). [SCOR S2.7 | source: build-new]
